items.rationale: Sees blockchains coordinating real-world services (compute, storage, cell service, energy) and frames a crisp investability test: a network works when verification cost falls below the incumbent's margin.

citations.quote: The essential question when considering building a DePIN network: Verification cost < incumbent margin big if true
Blockchains can provide any number of services, compute, storage, cell phone service, energy storage, physical transportation

items.rationale: He frames decentralized verification as the single hardest and most fundamental problem — crypto replaces trusted third parties with it, and everything else is an application of it.

citations.quote: Decentralized verification is the hardest problem and crux of why crypto matters
correctly verifying that a service has been provided/executed is almost always the hard problem when building a new decentralized network

items.rationale: Long-running conviction that ZKPs are underrated and transformational; recently extending to scaled L1 privacy and even proving LLM inference on-chain.
items.age_note: Thesis first stated 2022-01; restated and extended through 2023-02, so current, not stale.

citations.quote: i still think people underestimate how transformational zero knowledge proofs are
prove inference with validity proofs posted on Ethereum

items.rationale: Believes anyone should be able to proactively contribute to a protocol or DAO and be retroactively compensated in proportion to value created; calls it essential for long-term DAO success.

citations.quote: I'm very excited about the idea of permissionless contribution.
permissionless contribution is an idea i believe is essential for the long term success of daos

items.rationale: Strongly aligned with network- and value-level privacy as essential internet infrastructure; actively backs and champions projects like Nym and studies Aleo.

citations.quote: @nymproject will enable essential network level privacy for crypto, web3, and the broader Internet
It's fascinating to see scaled L1 privacy with ZKPs!

items.rationale: Frames tokens as multi-dimensional value carriers vs. scalar dollars, preserving design-space high dimensionality and enabling new human coordination.

citations.quote: Tokens are value vectors. Dollars are value scalars
We believe tokens are the native asset of the Internet, and they're enabling new methods of human coordination

items.rationale: Considers on-chain lending an essential DeFi primitive; led/championed Morpho's peer-to-peer matching atop pooled liquidity as a Pareto improvement in rates and capital efficiency.
items.age_note: Core conviction from 2022-07 investment; reaffirmed via 2022-11 integration praise, so persisted and still live.

citations.quote: on-chain lending is an essential DeFi primitive, but users must choose between pooled liquidity or full order books
Fantastic news that @MorphoLabs is now integrated with @Instadapp

items.rationale: Wants to move past 'not your keys, not your crypto' via MPC, smart-contract wallets, and policy-defined custody; backed Entropy to make secure self-sovereign custody accessible.
items.age_note: Stated around 2022-06 Entropy seed; not restated in 2023, so persisted at ~10 months old.

citations.quote: Entropy has the potential to bring decentralization to custody, allowing permissionless, secure, and efficient access to almost any token

items.rationale: Emerging excitement: crypto as the mechanism for distributing already-present AGI and aligning incentives at software speed; muses on token-coordinated AI-owned protocols.
items.age_note: Newest theme (2023-02/03), expressed speculatively/playfully rather than as firm thesis.

citations.quote: crypto is how we get along as the AGI that's already here becomes more evenly distributed
store an LLM on Filecoin/Arweave ... prove inference with validity proofs ... create a DAO to own/operate

items.rationale: Notes convergence of scaling approaches — L3s/hyperchains echoing appchains, restaking overlapping mesh security — across Ethereum and Cosmos.

citations.quote: L3s (also called hyper chains) can solve many of the same challenges as L1 appchains. Restaking and mesh security also seem to overlap

items.rationale: Predicts a 'metagame token' as the DeFi-governance equivalent for player-owned games, with on-chain logic enabling true decentralization.
items.age_note: Most developed in 2021-10 through 2022-04; not restated in 2023, so aging/persisted.

citations.quote: I believe the DeFi governance token equivalent for games will become the metagame token.
On-chain games could allow many different win conditions for different sets of players.

items.rationale: Worries that centralization at the networking layer lets DNS providers and ISPs censor large portions of most crypto networks — an under-addressed vulnerability.
items.age_note: Stated 2022-07; consistent with ongoing privacy/networking focus, treated as persisted concern.

citations.quote: centralization at the network level that would allow DNS providers and ISPs to censor large portions of most crypto networks

items.rationale: Even while excited about retroactive permissionless rewards, he flags that fairly assessing the value of a contribution after the fact is unsolved and gameable by malicious parties.

citations.quote: the open question for permissionless contribution is how to evaluate the quality and value created of a contribution after the fact

items.rationale: Repeatedly stresses decentralization is always less efficient than centralization; the cost must be justified and someone must bear it, which gates which projects can win.

citations.quote: Decentralization is always less efficient than centralization. Why is this cost worth paying, and who will bear it?

items.rationale: Concerned that with EIP-4844 blobs few validators actually persist data and instead query a small set of RPC providers, which is centralizing.
items.age_note: Raised 2022-05; not restated since, so aging.

citations.quote: If only a few nodes actually store blob data it's centralizing

items.rationale: Treats MEV as an unsettled core problem, framing the tension between market-based and privacy-based solutions without a clear resolution.

citations.quote: the bartender asks them how to solve MEV ... privacy can't prevent information asymmetry ... markets can't

items.rationale: Endorsed Solana as the venue for highest-throughput, atomically composable on-chain execution, specifically for fully on-chain games.
items.age_note: Single mention 2022-01-14, never restated; old and low-weight.

citations.quote: great to see some teams chose @solana for the highest throughput atomically composable execution environment

risk_regime.rationale: His posture is builder-optimism, not asset-price timing. He reads the early-2023 environment as a bottom-of-cycle building phase ('hard times create decentralized protocols'), called ETH Denver a 'whitepill', and explicitly advocates indulging optimism. He almost never makes price/trading calls; his risk-taking is conviction in long-horizon protocol/infrastructure theses (DePIN, ZK, decentralized verification) rather than near-term market direction.

notes: View synthesized from Guy Wuollet's a16z-crypto-aligned tweets; most-recent (2023 Q1) statements weighted highest. His public voice is thesis/infrastructure-oriented, not market-tactical — he rarely names specific tokens to long/short and makes no bearish token calls, hence the empty tokens_concerned and a single low-conviction persisted token (Solana). Strongest current excitement clusters around DePIN, decentralized verification, ZKPs, permissionless contribution, and tokens-as-value-vectors. Crypto x AI is the newest and most speculative thread (2023-02/03), expressed playfully. No material reversals detected: positions are additive over time; older themes (on-chain games/metagame tokens, blob DA centralization, custody) are marked persisted/aging because they were not restated in 2023. Everything is structural; he shows little tactical/trading horizon. All quotes truncated to <=25 words with ISO dates.
